notes: WIN-NAV-008. The framework status bar (vcl/source/window/status.cxx) and the generic per-app status controller compose the Material 28px status band guarded behind the Material file-widget theme; non-Material rendering, keyboard, mouse, a11y and RTL paths are untouched. definition.xml already carries the faceColor style slot, the band/field tokens and the zoom-slider parts from the Material foundations; this contract locks them against drift and binds them to the guarded native wiring. No native build, band pixels or runtime interaction are claimed.

field_hover.note: docs/design/05-navigation.md 8.2 marks the interactive-field hover wash (@primary-container, corner-small) as 'specified here' -- it is not yet a native part, and the status-bar container has no dedicated definition part (8.1). This section only grounds the tokens the field-hover spec depends on; the hover slot itself remains spec-only (runtime_verified false).

accessibility.note: docs/design/05-navigation.md 8.3/8.4: owner-drawn status updates must be exposed as accessible value changes, not bare repaints; the zoom readout stays textual, never value-by-position. Under the Material theme the generic controller routes the owner-drawn status string through the item's accessible name (SetAccessibleName -> StatusbarNameChanged -> VCLXAccessibleStatusBarItem NAME_CHANGED) in addition to the existing repaint. The accessible-name channel is used deliberately: unlike setText it does not touch maText or recompute the item width, so it cannot reflow the band or clip owner-drawn content and adds no second synchronous paint on top of repaint().
